laws.title: Fine as a Minor Penalty
laws.description: A fine may be imposed on an enrolled member of the Force for good and sufficient reasons as a minor penalty.[cite: 1]
laws.simpleExplanation: Enrolled members of the CISF can be fined a small portion of their salary as a punishment for misconduct.[cite: 1]

punishments.description: Fine to any amount not exceeding of 7 days pay.[cite: 1]

relatedSections.description: Defines the Disciplinary Authority competent to impose penalties.[cite: 1]
Relates to petty punishments for Head Constables, Constables, and Followers.[cite: 1]

laws.title: Petty Punishments
laws.description: Head Constable, Constable and Follower may also be awarded extra drill, guard, fatigue or other duty as punishment.[cite: 1]
laws.simpleExplanation: Lower-ranking members can be given extra duties or drills for minor disciplinary breaches.[cite: 1]

punishments.description: Extra drill, guard, fatigue or other duty for a term not exceeding fourteen days.[cite: 1]

relatedSections.description: Specifies that petty punishments are ordinarily awarded in the Orderly room.[cite: 1]

laws.title: Resignation and Training Cost Refund
laws.description: Members resigning within ten years of appointment for reasons other than a cleared job elsewhere must refund training costs or pay.[cite: 1]
laws.simpleExplanation: If you leave the Force early (within 10 years), you have to pay back the cost of your training or three months of salary.[cite: 1]

punishments.description: Refund the cost of training imparted or a sum equal to three months' pay and allowances, whichever is more.[cite: 1]

relatedSections.description: Relates to the agreement executed at the time of initial appointment.[cite: 1]
